title: baccharis prorepens
intro: Baccharis prorepens, also known as Button Willow or Creek Baccharis, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its compact growth and small, white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and protect against infections. The health benefits of Baccharis prorepens include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit antiseptic properties. Baccharis prorepens can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies to support overall health and well-being.
